text:
An Iran-related protest march that had been banned by the British government is due to go ahead in a different, more restricted form on Sunday. Organizers had originally planned to march through London for the annual pro-Palestinian event, known as Al-Quds Day and which was started by the first supreme leader of Iran following its 1979 revolution. The government prohibited the march, in a rare exercise of powers to prevent “serious disorder.” Organizers said they would instead hold the protest in one area, which the police call a “static protest.” Police said they plan to guard the event to prevent confrontations with counter-protesters.
In October 1973, oil-producing countries in the Middle East imposed an oil embargo, among other measures, on the United States and other countries that had supported Israel during the Yom Kippur war.
title: Britain warns its citizens in Dubai they could be jailed for sharing photos of damage from airstrikes.
